sections.heading: Problem Statement
sections.content: Structured documentation (DocJSON) is only useful if people can browse, edit, and maintain it without friction. Editing raw JSON files in a text editor is error-prone — malformed JSON, orphaned links, inconsistent tags. The doc manager provides an in-browser viewer and editor so developers and agents can maintain documentation alongside code without context-switching.

sections.heading: User Stories
sections.content:
**US-DM-01 — Browse docs by folder**
As a developer, I want to browse DocJSON files in a folder tree so I can find documentation by its location in the project.

**US-DM-02 — Filter docs by tag**
As a developer, I want to filter the doc list by tags so I can find all ADRs, all design specs, or all icebox items quickly.

**US-DM-03 — Edit sections inline**
As a developer, I want to edit a section's content directly in the browser so I don't have to open the raw JSON file and risk malforming it.

**US-DM-04 — Manage links to code nodes**
As a developer, I want to attach and detach links between doc sections and cortex nodes so the documentation stays connected to the code it describes.

**US-DM-05 — Create docs from the browser**
As a developer, I want to create a new DocJSON file (optionally from a template) without manually scaffolding the JSON structure.

**US-DM-06 — Edit Mermaid diagrams**
As a developer, I want a live-preview diagram editor so I can author and iterate on architecture diagrams without a separate tool.

sections.heading: Current Capabilities
sections.content:
| Capability | Status |
|---|---|
| Folder tree with expand/collapse persistence | Done |
| Tag filtering (multi-select, persisted in session) | Done |
| Rendered markdown view with Mermaid diagrams | Done |
| Click-to-edit sections (textarea) | Done |
| Inline heading editing | Done |
| Section add / remove / reorder | Done |
| Tag management (doc + section level) | Done |
| Link picker with node search | Done |
| Mermaid diagram editor (Monaco + live preview) | Done |
| Raw JSON source toggle | Done |
| Wide mode toggle | Done |
| Create new doc / folder | Done |
| Move / rename docs | Done |
| Markdown import | Done |
| Re-scan docs from disk | Done |

sections.heading: Backlog
sections.content:
**Hide empty folders when tag filter is active** — When tag filters are active, folders that contain no matching documents should be hidden from the sidebar tree. Currently empty folders remain visible, adding noise when browsing a filtered subset (e.g. all `icebox` docs, all `adr` docs).

**Rich-text editing (Tiptap)** — Replace the plain textarea with the Tiptap rich-text editor for a more polished editing experience. The Tiptap dependencies are already loaded in index.html but not yet wired into content editing.

**Template picker on new doc** — When creating a new document, offer a template selector populated from `docs/templates/`. Currently templates exist on disk but the UI doesn't surface them.

**Section drag-and-drop reorder** — Replace the current up/down arrow reorder buttons with drag-and-drop. Lower priority since the arrows work fine for small documents.

**Diff view on save** — Before saving, show a diff of what changed since the last save. Helps catch accidental edits.

**Tag color management** — Tags are currently all rendered as gray chips. Add a tag management panel where users can assign colors to tags (e.g. red for `todo`, blue for `adr`, orange for `icebox`). Colors should persist (localStorage or a `.cortex/tag-colors.json` file) and apply everywhere tags appear — sidebar filter checklist, doc-level chips, and section-level chips.
